source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A single-phase full-bridge inverter fed by a 325 V DC produces a symmetric quasi-square waveform across 'ab' as shown in <image>. To achieve a modulation index of 0.8, the angle \( \theta \) expressed in degrees should be ___________. (Round off to 2 decimal places.) (Modulation index is defined as the ratio of the peak of the fundamental component of \( V_{ab} \) to the applied DC value.)
extraction_notes: image field contains an in-memory PIL.Image representation, not a path/URL string

metadata.image_paths is empty while the top-level image_paths contains an image path.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says "as shown in the figure"
the unknown parameter is an angle theta tied to the depicted quasi-square waveform shape across ab, so the figure likely determines the waveform intervals/symmetry needed for solving
multi_step: solving requires identifying the waveform structure from the figure, obtaining the fundamental component expression, applying the modulation-index definition, and solving for theta
the problem combines circuit context, waveform interpretation, and a numeric target modulation index of 0.8
answer is rounded to 2 decimals, indicating a computed result rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a specific value (51.1)
question gives an explicit formula target: modulation index equals peak fundamental of Vab divided by applied DC value
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true, enabling downstream formula-based checking
